qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default attachment download routes allow unauthenticated path traversal into sibling local attachment roots
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a filesystem path-normalization flaw on `GET /download/:filename(*)` and `GET /dl/:param1/:param2/:filename(*)` that permits sibling attachment-root reads. The advisory instead describes stored XSS caused by permissive SVG preview classification in `isPreviewAllowed(...)` plus unsafe inline serving from `fileReadv3` on `/dltemp/:param(*)`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored-XSS root cause. The retained qualifying finding is an unauthenticated attachment-download traversal bug in different routes with a different trust-boundary failure.
evidence: The advisory root cause is a combination of permissive SVG MIME classification in `attachmentHelpers.ts` (`isPreviewAllowed(...)`) and unsafe inline file serving in `attachments.controller.ts` `fileReadv3` (`GET /dltemp/:param(*)`).

The only retained final qualifying finding, `attachment_download_path_traversal`, is tied to `GET /download/:filename(*)` and `GET /dl/:param1/:param2/:filename(*)`, where attacker-controlled paths are normalized against `${toolDir}/nc` instead of `${toolDir}/nc/uploads`.

The session manifest and retained-session summary list only `attachment_download_path_traversal` as a final qualifying finding; no final qualifying finding describes stored SVG upload, MIME-based preview allowance, or inline browser execution of uploaded content.
